text:
Advanced Java Programming
=========================

Overview:
Advanced Java focuses on enterprise-level application development, server-side technologies, and multithreaded programming. Students learn how to build scalable, secure, and interactive software using Servlets, JSP, JDBC, and networking concepts.

Units:
Unit 1 — Advanced OOP
- Inheritance, polymorphism, interfaces
- Java Collections Framework
- Generics and iterators
- Exception handling (custom exceptions)

Unit 2 — JDBC
- JDBC Architecture
- Types of drivers
- Connecting to MySQL/Oracle
- PreparedStatement vs Statement
- CRUD

andling (custom exceptions)

Unit 2 — JDBC
- JDBC Architecture
- Types of drivers
- Connecting to MySQL/Oracle
- PreparedStatement vs Statement
- CRUD operations
- Transaction control

Unit 3 — Servlets
- Servlet lifecycle
- GET & POST
- Session Tracking
- Cookies, URL Rewriting
- RequestDispatcher

Unit 4 — JSP
- JSP lifecycle
- Scriptlets, expressions
- Expression Language (EL)
- JSTL
- MVC architecture

Unit 5 — Threads & Networking
- Thread lifecycle
- Synchronization
- Inter-thread communication
- Socket programming (TCP/UDP)
- RMI

Applications:
- E-commerce backend
- Banking systems

lifecycle

Applications:

- Enterprise applications

Exam Topics:
- JDBC flow
- Servlet vs JSP
- MVC
- Thread states

Introduction to AIML (Oracle Academy)
=====================================

Overview:
This course introduces core AI and ML concepts with hands-on implementation using Oracle tools and Python. Students learn supervised and unsupervised learning, data preparation, and model evaluation.

Units:
Unit 1 — AI Basics
- History of AI
- Search techniques
- Heuristics

Unit 2 — Data & Preprocessing
- Data cleaning
- Feature engineering

Unit 3 — Supervised Learning
- Classification
- Regression
- Decision trees

Unit 4 — Unsupervised Learning
- Clustering
- Dimensionality reduction

Unit 5 — Neural

vised Learning
- Classification
- Regression
- Decision trees

Unit 4 — Unsupervised Learning
- Clustering
- Dimensionality reduction

Unit 5 — Neural Networks
- Perceptron model
- Activation functions

Unit 6 — Oracle Cloud AI Services
- Oracle AutoML
- Deployment

Applications:
- Recommendation systems
- Fraud detection
- Image & voice recognition

Aptitude
========

Overview:
Aptitude develops problem-solving skills essential for competitive exams and placements.

Units:
Unit 1 — Number Systems
Unit 2 — Percentages, Profit/Loss
Unit 3 — Algebra
Unit 4 — Probability
Unit 5 — Time & Work
Unit 6 — Logical Reasoning

Applications:
- Placement tests
- Bank exams
- Competitive exams

Computing Theory & Compiler Design
==================================

Overview:
This course covers automata theory and compiler construction. Students learn how programming languages are parsed and translated into machine code.

Units:
Unit 1 — Automata Theory
- DFA, NFA
- Regular expressions

Unit 2 — Context-Free Grammars
- Derivations
- Parse trees
- Ambiguity

Unit 3 — Lexical Analysis
- Tokenization
- Lexical errors
- Symbol table

Unit 4 — Syntax Analysis
- Top-down parsing
- Bottom-up parsing
- LR parsers

Unit 5 — Intermediate Code Generation
- TAC
- Syntax-directed translation

Unit

— Syntax Analysis
- Top-down parsing
- Bottom-up parsing
- LR parsers

Unit 5 — Intermediate Code Generation
- TAC
- Syntax-directed translation

Unit 6 — Optimization & Code Generation
- Peephole optimization

Computer Networks
=================

Overview:
Study of data communication principles, network models, protocols, and routing algorithms.

Units:
Unit 1 — Physical Layer
- Transmission media
- Modulation
- Bandwidth

Unit 2 — Data Link Layer
- Error detection
- Flow control
- MAC protocols

Unit 3 — Network Layer
- IP addressing
- Routing algorithms
- Subnetting

Unit 4 — Transport Layer
- TCP vs UDP
- Congestion control
- Reliability

Unit 5 — Application Layer
- HTTP, FTP, DNS
- Email protocols

Applications:
- Internet systems
- Cloud networks
- IoT devices

pplication Layer
- HTTP, FTP, DNS
- Email protocols

Applications:
- Internet systems
- Cloud networks
- IoT devices

Financial Management
====================

Overview:
Financial management focuses on planning, managing, and monitoring financial resources of a business.

Units:
Unit 1 — Introduction to Finance
- Goals of FM
- Time value of money

Unit 2 — Capital Budgeting
- NPV, IRR
- Payback methods

Unit 3 — Working Capital
- Cash, inventory, receivables

Unit 4 — Cost of Capital
- Debt vs equity
- WACC

Unit 5 — Risk & Return
- CAPM model

Applications:
- Banking
- Investments
- Corporate management

Software Engineering
====================

Overview:
Software Engineering deals with systematic development, design, testing, and maintenance of software systems. It emphasizes process models, design techniques, and quality assurance.

Units:
Unit 1 — Introduction
- Software crisis
- SDLC models: Waterfall, Spiral, Agile
- Requirements engineering

Unit 2 — System Design
- ER diagrams
- DFDs
- Architectural styles
- Cohesion & coupling

Unit 3 — Implementation
- Coding standards
- Version control
- Debugging and refactoring

Unit 4 — Testing
- Unit testing
- Integration testing
- System

it 3 — Implementation
- Coding standards
- Version control
- Debugging and refactoring

Unit 4 — Testing
- Unit testing
- Integration testing
- System testing
- Test case design

Unit 5 — Maintenance & SCM
- Types of maintenance
- Software configuration management

Applications:
- IT companies
- Product development
- Large team projects

Exam Topics:
- SDLC models
- DFD levels
- Cohesion/Coupling
